headlines.title: SoftBank shares surge over 16% as Japan tech-fueled rally lifts Nikkei 225 to record highs
Japan says it has no limits on yen intervention, in daily contact with US By Reuters
Nikkei Surges past 62,000 amid Middle East Hopes
Japan says it has no limits on yen intervention, in daily contact with US
Nikkei Surges Past 62,000 As Tech Rally And Peace Hopes Lift Japan Markets
Nikkei 225 Breakout: Japan Stocks Target 65,000 as Risk Appetite Returns
Japan’s Nikkei hits record intraday high on US-Iran deal hopes

marketTone.ko: 일본 증시는 기술주 랠리와 중동 평화 기대감에 힘입어 사상 최고치를 경신하며 강세를 보였습니다. 소프트뱅크 주가가 16% 이상 급등하는 등 기술주가 시장 상승을 주도하며 닛케이 225 지수는 62,000선을 돌파하고 65,000선까지 목표하고 있습니다. 한편, 일본 정부는 엔화 개입에 제한이 없으며 미국과 매일 소통하고 있다고 밝혀 시장의 주목을 받았습니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese stock market experienced a strong rally today, with the Nikkei 225 surging to record highs, significantly driven by a tech-fueled rally that saw SoftBank shares jump over 16%. Optimism surrounding Middle East peace hopes and potential US-Iran deal hopes also contributed to the market's robust performance, pushing the Nikkei past 62,000 and targeting 65,000. Amidst this bullish sentiment, Japan reiterated its stance on yen intervention, stating it has no limits and is in daily contact with the US.
